recipe.title: Proper Scotch eggs
recipe.description: A cracking recipe that shows you how to make perfect scotch eggs from scratch. Perfect for a picnic or a party. Just add crunchy pickles and some salad.

ingredients.name: ½ a bunch of fresh chives
½ a bunch of fresh flat-leaf parsley

steps.descr: Put 8 eggs into a pan of cold water and bring to the boil
steps.instructions: Put 8 eggs into a pan of cold water and bring to the boil. Boil for 3 to 4 minutes, then transfer to a bowl of cold water. Once cooled, carefully peel them.

on_enter.say: Put 8 eggs into a pan of cold water and bring to the boil. Boil for 3 to 4 minutes, then transfer to a bowl of cold water. Once cooled, carefully peel them.

steps.descr: Squeeze the sausages out of their skins and into a bowl
steps.instructions: Squeeze the sausages out of their skins and into a bowl.

on_enter.say: Squeeze the sausages out of their skins and into a bowl.

steps.descr: Pick and finely chop the herb leaves, add to the sausage...
steps.instructions: Pick and finely chop the herb leaves, add to the sausage meat along with a good grating of nutmeg, the mustard and a good pinch of sea salt and black pepper. Give it all a good mix together then divide into 8 balls.

on_enter.say: Pick and finely chop the herb leaves, add to the sausage meat along with a good grating of nutmeg, the mustard and a good pinch of sea salt and black pepper. Give it all a good mix together then divide into 8 balls.

steps.descr: Have 3 plates ready - one with a small handful of flour,...
steps.instructions: Have 3 plates ready - one with a small handful of flour, one with the two remaining eggs (beat them together first), and a third with the breadcrumbs.

on_enter.say: Have 3 plates ready - one with a small handful of flour, one with the two remaining eggs (beat them together first), and a third with the breadcrumbs.

steps.descr: Start by flouring your hands, then in the palm of one...
steps.instructions: Start by flouring your hands, then in the palm of one hand, flatten one of the sausage balls into an oval-shaped pattie.

on_enter.say: Start by flouring your hands, then in the palm of one hand, flatten one of the sausage balls into an oval-shaped pattie.

steps.descr: Roll a peeled egg in flour, then pop it in the middle of...
steps.instructions: Roll a peeled egg in flour, then pop it in the middle of the patty Gently shape the meat evenly around the egg, moulding it with your hands until sealed.

on_enter.say: Roll a peeled egg in flour, then pop it in the middle of the patty Gently shape the meat evenly around the egg, moulding it with your hands until sealed.

steps.descr: Roll the meat-wrapped egg in the flour, shake off any...
steps.instructions: Roll the meat-wrapped egg in the flour, shake off any excess, then dip into the beaten egg, followed by the breadcrumbs. Roll in the egg and breadcrumbs again for a really good coating.

on_enter.say: Roll the meat-wrapped egg in the flour, shake off any excess, then dip into the beaten egg, followed by the breadcrumbs. Roll in the egg and breadcrumbs again for a really good coating.

steps.descr: Heat the oil in a deep pan or deep fat fryer to about...
steps.instructions: Heat the oil in a deep pan or deep fat fryer to about 150ºC/300ºF. If you have a cooking thermometer it’s a good idea to use it. Otherwise, test if the oil is hot enough by adding a piece of raw potato and leaving it for about 1 minute – if it sizzles and browns, it’s ready.

on_enter.say: Heat the oil in a deep pan or deep fat fryer to about 150ºC/300ºF. If you have a cooking thermometer it’s a good idea to use it. Otherwise, test if the oil is hot enough by adding a piece of raw potato and leaving it for about 1 minute – if it sizzles and browns, it’s ready.

steps.descr: Carefully lower the eggs into the pan and cook for 4...
steps.instructions: Carefully lower the eggs into the pan and cook for 4 minutes, or until golden, turning them every so often. Remove with a slotted spoon and drain on kitchen paper (if you’re worried about the meat being under-cooked, deep-fry the scotch eggs until they’re golden and crispy, then pop them in a hot oven for a couple of minutes.)

on_enter.say: Carefully lower the eggs into the pan and cook for 4 minutes, or until golden, turning them every so often. Remove with a slotted spoon and drain on kitchen paper (if you’re worried about the meat being under-cooked, deep-fry the scotch eggs until they’re golden and crispy, then pop them in a hot oven for a couple of minutes.)

steps.descr: Cool the Scotch eggs slightly, then arrange on board...
steps.instructions: Cool the Scotch eggs slightly, then arrange on board with a good piece of Scottish Cheddar, some pickle and a few pickled onions. Heaven.

on_enter.say: Cool the Scotch eggs slightly, then arrange on board with a good piece of Scottish Cheddar, some pickle and a few pickled onions. Heaven.
